THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME, $ W_f $, CAN BE EXPRESSED AS $ W_f = W \times (1 - 0.02)^n $. THIS FORMULA ACCOUNTS FOR THE 2% WEIGHT REDUCTION PER ADVANCED COMPONENT ADDED TO THE FRAME.

TO ACHIEVE A 25% REDUCTION IN ENERGY EXPENDITURE, THE FUNCTION $ E(n) = 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n $ MUST EQUAL OR EXCEED 0.25. SOLVING THIS INEQUALITY, WE GET $ 1 - (0.98)^n \geq 0.25 $, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO $ (0.98)^n \leq 0.75 $. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES, $ n \ln(0.98) \leq \ln(0.75) $. SOLVING FOR $ n $, WE FIND $ n \geq \frac{\ln(0.75)}{\ln(0.98)} \approx 13.5 $. SINCE $ n $ MUST BE AN INTEGER, THE MINIMUM NUMBER OF COMPONENTS IS 14. THE CLOCK TICKS ONWARD AS TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE. THE CLOCK SHOWS THAT PRECISION IS CRITICAL. THE CLOCK REMINDS US THAT EVERY SECOND COUNTS IN A LONG-DISTANCE EVENT.
